title: RFK Jr. Launches Presidential Campaign
description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. formally declares his candidacy for the Democratic presidential nomination at the Park Plaza Hotel in Boston. His campaign centers on ending the 'corrupt merger of state and corporate power,' chronic disease, and food safety — the ideological seeds of MAHA.

title: RFK Jr. Relaunches as Independent Candidate
description: Kennedy ends his Democratic primary challenge and re-enters the race as an independent at a rally in Philadelphia. His platform increasingly emphasizes food systems, toxic chemicals, and the chronic disease epidemic — positioning MAHA-style ideas as a cross-partisan cause.

title: The MAHA Slogan Is Born
description: The phrase "Make America Healthy Again" officially enters the political lexicon, championed by Kennedy's campaign. The movement draws from the "health freedom" movement that grew during the COVID-19 pandemic, uniting clean food advocates and critics of Big Pharma, Big Agriculture, and corporate capture of federal health agencies.

title: RFK Jr. Suspends Campaign & Endorses Trump
description: Kennedy suspends his independent presidential bid at a press conference in Phoenix and endorses Donald Trump, uniting the MAHA coalition with MAGA — marrying health freedom politics with conservative electoral power. Kennedy delivers a bloc of voters focused on food safety, chronic disease, and reform of federal health agencies.

title: Sen. Ron Johnson Announces 'A Second Opinion' Roundtable
description: Sen. Ron Johnson (R-WI) issues a media advisory announcing a nonpartisan roundtable titled "American Health and Nutrition: A Second Opinion," to be held September 23 in the Russell Senate Office Building. The event features Dr. Casey Means, Calley Means, Jordan Peterson, Vani Hari (Food Babe), Jillian Michaels, and other MAHA-aligned voices.

title: Johnson Roundtable Held — Full Video Released
description: The Senate roundtable "American Health and Nutrition: A Second Opinion" takes place in the Kennedy Caucus Room at the Russell Senate Office Building. The full session is livestreamed and later released publicly. It signals growing congressional interest in MAHA ideas ahead of the election.

title: Trump Brings RFK Jr. Onstage at Campaign Rally
description: Trump welcomes Kennedy to the stage at a Turning Point Action rally in Duluth, Georgia, cementing the MAHA-MAGA alliance before a national audience and signaling Kennedy's future role in a potential Trump administration.

title: Trump Elected 47th President
description: Donald Trump wins the presidency. MAHA-aligned voters are credited as a meaningful coalition contribution, particularly independents and former Democrats who crossed over based on health and food safety concerns.

title: Trump Nominates RFK Jr. to Lead HHS
description: President-elect Trump nominates Kennedy as Secretary of Health and Human Services, giving MAHA its highest-ever institutional foothold. The nomination draws bipartisan scrutiny in the Senate over Kennedy's views on food safety, pharmaceutical regulation, and the role of federal health agencies.

title: Senate MAHA Caucus Launched
description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-KS) launches the Make America Healthy Again Caucus in the Senate to support the joint mission of Trump and Kennedy. It represents the formal legislative arm of the movement on Capitol Hill.

title: FDA Bans Red Dye No. 3
description: In the final days of the Biden administration, the FDA revokes authorization for Red Dye No. 3 (FD&C Red No. 3) — one of the first concrete regulatory actions aligned with MAHA's emphasis on food ingredient safety. Food manufacturers have until January 2027 to comply.

title: RFK Jr. Confirmation Hearings
description: Kennedy appears before the Senate Finance Committee and the HELP Committee. His views on pharmaceutical regulation and federal health agency reform draw bipartisan scrutiny. Sen. Mitch McConnell is among those voicing concerns. 'MAHA moms' flood Senate phone lines in support.

title: RFK Jr. Confirmed as HHS Secretary (52–48)
description: The Senate confirms Kennedy along party lines, 52–48. The same day, President Trump signs an executive order establishing the Make America Healthy Again Commission, chaired by Kennedy. The commission is directed to examine childhood chronic disease, ultra-processed foods, environmental chemicals, and overmedicalization.

title: DOGE Targets HHS — Mass Layoffs Begin
description: DOGE teams begin reviewing HHS contracts and grants. Approximately 10,000 HHS employees depart through voluntary separation. A second wave of 10,000 layoff notices follows in late March–April, cutting roughly 20–31% of the federal public health workforce across FDA, CDC, and NIH. Critics warn this undercuts MAHA's own stated goals.

title: West Virginia Bans Seven Synthetic Dyes
description: West Virginia becomes the first state to ban seven synthetic food dyes under a new law signed by the governor. The bill is sponsored by a Republican legislator motivated by his foster children's health. Around the same time, roughly 75 dye-related bills are introduced across 37 states.

title: HHS Claws Back $11.4 Billion in CDC Grants
description: HHS confirms the clawback of $11.4 billion in COVID-era funds from state and local health departments. Public health clinics are canceled across the country amid a measles outbreak in Texas that kills two children. Critics argue the cuts directly contradict MAHA's child health goals.

title: Mass HHS Layoffs Execute — FDA, CDC, NIH Hit Hard
description: Termination notices go out to thousands of HHS staffers. At the FDA, 3,500 jobs are eliminated, including top regulatory officials. Entire offices — communications, FOIA, infertility research — are shuttered. Former FDA leaders across both parties warn that institutional capacity has been irreparably damaged.

title: FDA Announces Phase-Out of All Synthetic Food Dyes
description: Kennedy and FDA Commissioner Marty Makary announce a plan to eliminate all petroleum-based synthetic dyes from the U.S. food supply by end of 2026. The FDA will revoke authorization for dyes not in production and establish timelines for remaining dyes. Major companies including Nestlé, Hershey, and PepsiCo pledge to comply.

title: MAHA Commission Report Criticized for Fake Citations
description: The Democratic National Committee and independent analysts flag multiple citations in a draft MAHA commission report as nonexistent — evidence of AI-generated sourcing errors. The White House calls them 'formatting issues.' The episode damages the report's credibility and fuels broader criticism of the commission's rigor.

title: MAHA Institute Launches in Washington
description: Kennedy campaign veterans Mark Gorton and Tony Lyons formally launch the MAHA Institute in Washington, D.C. — rebranding the 501(c)(4) wing of their American Values Super PAC into a permanent policy and advocacy organization. Described as a rival to established think tanks, the Institute is designed to translate grassroots MAHA energy into legislative and regulatory action. Its stated mission: connect the movement's 110,000+ supporters with the roughly 75 true MAHA allies inside a 60,000-person HHS bureaucracy. Calley Means, White House health adviser, calls it a 'generational opportunity.' Zen Honeycutt of Moms Across America and Mary Holland of Children's Health Defense are among the founding coalition.

title: MAHA-MAGA Rift Opens Over EPA Pesticide Policy
description: EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin moves to continue allowing dicamba, expand PFAS-containing pesticides, and resist pesticide restrictions — directly contradicting MAHA priorities. MAHA activists circulate a petition demanding Trump fire Zeldin. The EPA's posture is widely described as 'misaligned' with HHS and USDA.

title: House Appropriations Pesticide Rider Ignites MAHA Backlash
description: A House appropriations bill for the EPA includes language that would bar states and courts from penalizing pesticide makers for failing to include health warnings beyond those recognized by the EPA — effectively shielding Roundup/glyphosate maker Bayer from failure-to-warn lawsuits. MAHA groups call it a 'liability shield.'

title: MAHA Commission Releases 'Make Our Children Healthy Again' Strategy
description: HHS releases the commission's strategy report with more than 120 initiatives to reverse the childhood chronic disease crisis, covering diet, environmental chemicals, physical activity, and overmedicalization. Critics note the report omits calls to reduce pesticide exposure — reportedly after EPA officials intervened.

title: MAHA Summit at Waldorf Astoria — VP Vance Attends
description: A high-profile invitation-only MAHA summit is held at the Waldorf Astoria in Washington. VP JD Vance attends and calls MAHA 'a critical part of our success in Washington.' The event signals the movement's continued relevance to GOP electoral strategy ahead of 2026 midterms.

title: EPA Approves Second 'Forever Chemical' Pesticide in Two Weeks
description: The Trump EPA approves isocycloseram — a PFAS 'forever chemical' pesticide — for use on golf courses, lawns, and food crops including oranges, tomatoes, almonds, and oats. It is the second PFAS pesticide approved in two weeks, with three more planned. The EPA waives a standard child-safety buffer despite the pesticide's links to reduced sperm count, reduced testicle size, and liver toxicity. The pesticide office is controlled by two former lobbyists for the American Chemistry Council. Critics note the approvals directly contradict MAHA's stated mission to protect children from toxic chemical exposures.

title: 'Eat Real Food' Campaign Launches with New Dietary Guidelines
description: Kennedy and Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins unveil the Dietary Guidelines for Americans 2025–2030 at a White House briefing, with Kennedy declaring it 'the most significant reset of federal nutrition policy in history.' The new guidelines introduce a redesigned, inverted food pyramid placing protein, dairy, and healthy fats at the top. For the first time, federal dietary guidance explicitly warns Americans to avoid highly processed foods. The message is distilled to three words: 'Eat real food.' A companion website, RealFood.gov, goes live to reach the public directly.

title: 'Take Back Your Health' / 'Eat Real Food' Tour Kicks Off in Pennsylvania
description: Kennedy launches a national 'Take Back Your Health' tour, beginning at the Pennsylvania State Capitol in Harrisburg — the first stop of what his office brands an 'Eat Real Food Tour.' He joins over a dozen state legislators to promote the new food pyramid, dietary guidelines, and MAHA's state-by-state legislative agenda. The tour is designed as a hybrid of public health outreach and political messaging, rallying state capitals ahead of the 2026 midterms.

title: Mike Tyson Joins HHS 'Eat Real Food' Campaign
description: Kennedy posts a video to his official HHS social media accounts featuring former heavyweight champion Mike Tyson slapping a donut out of a staffer's hand and declaring, 'What do you think you're doing?! Processed food KILLS.' Tyson, whose sister died at 25 from obesity-related heart failure, tells Fox News he joined willingly: 'I'm one of the most healthiest people on the planet — they should think I will want to be a participant in this.' The clip goes viral, racking up millions of views. The partnership cements Tyson as an unofficial celebrity ambassador for MAHA's 'Eat Real Food' message.

title: Mike Tyson Stars in MAHA Super Bowl Ad
description: The MAHA Center — an advocacy group aligned with Kennedy's HHS agenda — airs a 30-second Super Bowl ad starring Mike Tyson. In the spot, Tyson recounts weighing 345 pounds and consuming 'a quart of ice cream every hour,' and shares that his sister died at 25 from a heart attack linked to obesity. The ad ends with Tyson and his son eating apples, directing viewers to RealFood.gov. It is one of the Super Bowl's most counter-programming moments — a federal health message during America's biggest junk food occasion.

title: WIN: Pesticide Liability Rider Stripped from EPA Spending Bill
description: After a 'hard fight,' the controversial Bayer-backed pesticide liability provision is removed from the 2026 EPA appropriations bill. Rep. Chellie Pingree (D-ME) credits MAHA-aligned advocates — including Moms Across America founder Zen Honeycutt — with providing critical cross-party pressure on Republican lawmakers. Senate Republicans agree not to revisit it.

title: Trump Signs Glyphosate Executive Order — MAHA Outrage
description: President Trump invokes the Defense Production Act to compel domestic production of glyphosate-based herbicides, declaring them critical to national security. MAHA advocates are furious. Zen Honeycutt of Moms Across America calls it 'a love letter to glyphosate.' Food activist Vani Hari says it 'reads like it was drafted in a chemical company boardroom.'

title: RFK Jr. Backs Trump on Glyphosate as National Security Issue
description: Kennedy publicly defends Trump's glyphosate executive order, framing it as a matter of national security and agricultural supply chain resilience. In a lengthy social media post he acknowledges pesticides are 'toxic by design' and commits to accelerating the transition to regenerative agriculture — but backs the order as a necessary step given U.S. dependence on foreign-controlled inputs. 'I support President Trump's Executive Order to bring agricultural chemical production back to the United States and end our near-total reliance on adversarial nations,' Kennedy writes. He positions the EO as consistent with the long-term MAHA goal of building a safer food system, promising federal urgency on alternatives including precision weed control, robotics, and biological controls.

title: EPA Reapproves Dicamba Despite Court Rulings and Drift Damage
description: The Trump EPA reapproves dicamba — a drift-prone herbicide sprayed on genetically engineered cotton and soybeans — despite federal courts having ruled such approvals unlawful in both 2020 and 2024. Since its first approval in 2016, dicamba drift has damaged millions of acres of farmland, orchards, vegetable farms, and wildlife refuges. Experts call it the worst herbicide drift damage in U.S. agricultural history. The new approval provides even fewer drift protections than past versions. Lawsuits are filed within weeks.

title: EPA Plans to Loosen Mercury Rules for Coal Plants
description: The New York Times reports that the EPA is moving to roll back mercury emission rules for coal-burning power plants, arguing it would reduce 'unwarranted costs' for utilities. Mercury from coal plants is a major source of fish contamination — the same issue RFK Jr. has long prioritized. The administration has simultaneously ordered eight coal units headed for retirement to stay open. The move draws sharp criticism from MAHA-aligned health advocates as a direct contradiction of Kennedy's anti-mercury agenda.

title: Trump Administration Backs Bayer at Supreme Court
description: The Trump administration sides with Bayer-Monsanto in a Supreme Court case that could severely limit individuals' ability to sue if they believe they got cancer from glyphosate-based pesticides. MAHA advocates call it a betrayal. 'MAHA moms' warn this could be a breaking point for the movement.

title: MAHA Action Sends GOP Strategy Memo: 'A Once in a Generation Political Gift'
description: MAHA Action President Tony Lyons sends a formal strategy memo to RNC, NRSC, and NRCC chairmen — cc'd to Senate Majority Leader John Thune and Speaker Mike Johnson — arguing that MAHA is 'a once in a generation political gift to the GOP,' comparable to MAGA in 2016. New polling from Fabrizio, Lee and Associates identifies two key voter blocs the GOP must activate: the 'MAHA Winnable Middle' (14% of voters — persuadable Democrats and independents who chose a MAHA-framed Republican over a generic Democrat by nearly 2:1) and 'MAHA Rentals' (10% — soft Republicans who back the generic GOP but may stay home if the party ignores MAHA). The memo warns that without embracing MAHA, Republicans risk suppressing friendly turnout, and argues the movement can flip a 5-point generic ballot Democratic lead into a Republican advantage. Top persuasion issues: hospital price transparency, removing petroleum-based food dyes, testing baby formula for heavy metals, and restricting misleading marketing targeting children.

title: RFK Jr. & Kid Rock 'Rock Out Workout' Video — Peak MAHA Cringe
description: Kennedy posts a 90-second official HHS video teaming up with Kid Rock to 'GET ACTIVE + EAT REAL FOOD': the pair go shirtless in an infrared sauna, Kennedy cold-plunges in his jeans, they play pickleball, and conclude by drinking whole milk in a grotto pool to Kid Rock's 'Bawitdaba' — with a stuffed bear in a bedazzled fedora watching over them. The clip racks up 11.5 million views. Even MAHA supporters struggle to explain it. (Historical footnote: at the same time, Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show was drawing 128 million viewers. Kid Rock's competing event drew 6 million. The jeans got wet for this.)

title: RFK Jr. Action Figure Ad Goes Viral — 'Iron Mike Tyson' Edition
description: Kennedy posts an AI-generated retro action figure commercial to his official HHS account that racks up over 2 million views. The spot opens with a fireball and the line: 'With chronic disease running rampant, President Trump is sending one man.' The Kennedy action figure battles a 'Chronic Disease' Godzilla monster, drinks whole milk, flips the new food pyramid, and dives into a river in 'waterproof jeans.' The ad closes with a 'limited edition Iron Mike Tyson' figure sliding into frame — Tyson's robotic voice intoning, 'Eat real food or else.' HHS creative director Ryan Nahill, who produced the spot, posts: 'At HHS, we're breaking the internet with one simple message: EAT REAL FOOD.' The video draws both viral mockery and fervent praise from MAHA supporters.

title: MAHA Voters Begin Souring — Midterm Warning Signs
description: Bloomberg and CNBC report that key MAHA constituencies — particularly independents and former Democrats — are growing disillusioned with the Trump administration over pesticide policies and the EPA's posture on chemicals. 'Glyphosate Girl' Kelly Ryerson warns: 'These are not loyal Republican voters. They came here because they believed this administration was finally going to stand up to chemical companies. If that doesn't happen, they will absolutely walk.'

title: MAHA Rally for Real Food — Austin, Texas
description: Kennedy keynotes the MAHA Rally for Real Food in Austin. He frames federal procurement — $405M/day for school lunches, WIC, SNAP, and Head Start — as the primary lever to reshape the food system. He touts the GRAS loophole closure, the new food pyramid, and the food dye phase-out as major wins.

title: House GOP Farm Bill Revives Pesticide Liability Shield
description: House Republicans include nearly identical pesticide-protection language in the new farm bill — preventing states and courts from penalizing pesticide makers for health warnings beyond EPA recognition. MAHA allies are outraged that the provision stripped from the appropriations bill has resurfaced. The Environmental Working Group calls it a 'betrayal of MAHA.' Republican leadership stands by the measure despite pressure from the movement.

title: Trump Proposes Rolling Back Offshore Oil Decommissioning Bonds
description: The Trump Interior Department proposed replacing a Biden-era rule that required oil and gas companies to post $6.9 billion in bonds to cover offshore platform cleanup costs in the event of bankruptcy. The new rule would save the industry $484 million per year in compliance costs. A 2024 GAO report had found taxpayers exposed to billions in risk if offshore operators fail to meet decommissioning obligations — the rollback shifts that liability back to the public.
